verses.text: The leaders — Nehemiah, the priests, Levites, and chief men — set their names and seals to the covenant. The heads commit themselves first and in writing. When those in authority bind themselves openly to God, they lead the people into faithfulness.

The leaders — Nehemiah, the priests, Levites, and chief men — set their names and seals to the covenant. The heads commit themselves first and in writing. When those in authority bind themselves openly to God, they lead the people into faithfulness.

The rest of the people, with their wives and children old enough to understand, join their leaders, entering 'into a curse, and into an oath, to walk in God's law.' All who can understand take the vow. A covenant with God is for every soul able to grasp it, not the leaders alone.

The rest of the people, with their wives and children old enough to understand, join their leaders, entering 'into a curse, and into an oath, to walk in God's law.' All who can understand take the vow. A covenant with God is for every soul able to grasp it, not the leaders alone.

They pledge not to give their daughters to the surrounding peoples nor take their daughters for their sons. They guard the home against corrupting alliances. Faithfulness to God begins with keeping the family from unequal, idol-bearing bonds.

They vow not to buy on the sabbath or holy day, and to let the land rest every seventh year and release debts. They honour God's times and His poor. Reverence for the sabbath and mercy in money are both marks of a covenant heart.

They charge themselves yearly to support the temple service — the shewbread, offerings, and all the work of God's house. They tax themselves for worship. Those who value God's house will gladly bear the cost of keeping its service alive.

They charge themselves yearly to support the temple service — the shewbread, offerings, and all the work of God's house. They tax themselves for worship. Those who value God's house will gladly bear the cost of keeping its service alive.

By lot they arrange the wood offering, and pledge their firstfruits, firstborn, and tithes to the house of God and its ministers. They give God the first and best. Covenant faithfulness shows itself in bringing the firstfruits, not the leftovers, to the Lord.

By lot they arrange the wood offering, and pledge their firstfruits, firstborn, and tithes to the house of God and its ministers. They give God the first and best. Covenant faithfulness shows itself in bringing the firstfruits, not the leftovers, to the Lord.

By lot they arrange the wood offering, and pledge their firstfruits, firstborn, and tithes to the house of God and its ministers. They give God the first and best. Covenant faithfulness shows itself in bringing the firstfruits, not the leftovers, to the Lord.

The Levites are to tithe their tithes, and all bring their offerings to the chambers, resolving, 'we will not forsake the house of our God.' Their pledge ends in a settled vow. The proof of devotion is a people who will not neglect the worship and upkeep of God's house.
